commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3157ed62287b87edc2b2841613a0c7e587ec3bfd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds agent execution-packet guardrails for `sessions_spawn` and cron `agentTurn`, new script checks and LOC/dependency metadata guardrails, prompt snapshot refreshes, and related tests.

PR surface: Source +192, Tests +796, Config +1, Other +679. Total +1668 across 29 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection at PR head shows `scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` still resolves `node_modules/.bin/tsgo`, and `git grep` shows the new helper is only used by its test while the resource-guard marker is not consumed before spawning tsgo.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can address the narrow runner wiring and resource-guard defects; compatibility acceptance and final conflict review remain maintainer-visible gates.

target.title: chore(agent): port audit guardrails to mainline
target.body:
## Latest landing status (2026-05-26)

- Current head: `3157ed62287b87edc2b2841613a0c7e587ec3bfd`.
- GitHub reported this PR as `MERGEABLE` / `CLEAN` before this metadata-only body refresh.
- Current code checks are passing or intentionally skipped on the current head; this body update may briefly re-run proof/metadata gates.
- `maintainerCanModify` is enabled for the fork branch.
- GitHub rejects reviewer requests from `chkh6` with a `RequestReviewsByLogin` permission failure.
- Remaining blocker: a maintainer/write-permission actor must merge the PR, request final review from the maintainer side, or grant temporary write permission to `chkh6` for landing.

## Summary

- Port the approved agent-system audit hardening onto current `origin/main`.
- Add runtime execution-packet linting for side-effectful `sessions_spawn` and cron `agentTurn` work.
- Harden self-optimization checks around LOC concurrency/baselines, tsgo invocation/resource guards, dependency ownership coverage, and prompt snapshot generation.
- Address follow-up review blockers by exempting ordinary read-only source reviews and committing regenerated prompt snapshots for the updated cron / `sessions_spawn` schemas.
- Normalize prompt snapshot model fixture paths so Linux and Windows generation no longer drift.
- Align the doctor legacy-config migration test mock with the real plugin manifest registry shape so the Linux doctor shard no longer fails when metadata memoization clones diagnostics.

## Validation

Latest local validation was run on Windows from `D:\OpenClaw-state\worktrees\agent-system-optimization-port-20260514` after the final PR fixes.

- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` - passed, 7 files current.
- `pnpm test -- test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts` - passed, 10 tests.
- `pnpm test -- src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts` - passed, 49 tests.
- `pnpm test -- src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts` - passed, 26 tests.
- `git diff --check` - passed.

Previously validated in this branch after the main rebase:

- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

GitHub CI on head `3157ed62287b87edc2b2841613a0c7e587ec3bfd` passed the previously blocking lanes including `check-additional-boundaries-a`, `checks-node-agentic-commands-doctor`, `check-lint`, `check-prod-types`, `check-test-types`, `build-artifacts`, `check-guards`, `checks-windows-node-test`, and the critical/security boundary checks before this body-only refresh.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Side-effectful delegated cron `agentTurn` runtime packets are exposed in schema, enforced before execution, and stripped from cron add/update forwarding, including the accepted `executionPacket`, `runtimePacket`, and `boundedExecutionPacket` aliases. The follow-up fixes also stabilize prompt snapshot paths and the doctor metadata mock shape so the same behavior can pass Linux CI gates.
- Real environment tested: Local Windows OpenClaw worktree `D:\OpenClaw-state\worktrees\agent-system-optimization-port-20260514`, current PR head `3157ed62287b87edc2b2841613a0c7e587ec3bfd`, with the existing dependency cache at `D:\OpenClaw\node_modules`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `NODE_PATH=D:\OpenClaw\node_modules node --import tsx --input-type=module -` with an inline probe importing `./src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts`, creating `createCronTool`, invoking `cron.add` and `cron.update` for `executionPacket`, `runtimePacket`, and `boundedExecutionPacket`, then inspecting the exact params forwarded to the gateway call boundary. After the snapshot and doctor follow-ups, the branch was pushed and GitHub re-ran the same PR head through the CI merge gates.
- Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the local runtime probe:

```text
$ NODE_PATH=D:\OpenClaw\node_modules node --import tsx --input-type=module -
{
  "remoteHead": "3157ed62287b87edc2b2841613a0c7e587ec3bfd",
  "aliases": [
    "executionPacket",
    "runtimePacket",
    "boundedExecutionPacket"
  ],
  "callCount": 6,
  "methods": [
    "cron.add",
    "cron.update",
    "cron.add",
    "cron.update",
    "cron.add",
    "cron.update"
  ],
  "forwardedAliasHits": [],
  "allAliasesStripped": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The live output shows all three accepted packet aliases were exercised through both `cron.add` and `cron.update`; no alias remained at the forwarded top level, payload, patch, or patch payload level; and `allAliasesStripped` was `true`. GitHub then validated the current head through the relevant PR checks after the Linux snapshot-drift and doctor-shard blockers were fixed.
- What was not tested: Full clean `pnpm install`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, and `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` were not rerun from a fresh dependency install in this port worktree because registry/native optional package availability had previously been blocked for `@typescript/native-preview-win32-x64@7.0.0-dev.20260511.1`. The targeted local validations and GitHub CI checks above cover the touched surfaces.

## Notes

- This PR does not modify constitution, governance rules, or durable memory.
- The fork branch allows maintainer edits (`maintainerCanModify=true`).
- The contributor account `chkh6` currently has pull-only access to `openclaw/openclaw`, so final landing still requires a maintainer/write-permission actor.

commands.author: SebTardif

target.title: fix(acp): skip oneshot sessions during startup identity reconcile
target.body:
Fixes #72013

## Summary

Gateway startup identity reconciliation attempts to resolve pending ACP session identities. Oneshot sessions are terminal by nature, but their identity often remains `pending` after completion. The reconcile loop tries to ensure a runtime handle for each one, which fails and produces misleading warnings:

```text
[gateway] acp startup identity reconcile (renderer=v1): checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7
```

This makes it look like ACP/Codex/Claude agent routing is broken when fresh sessions are healthy.

## Fix

Skip sessions with `mode === "oneshot"` in `runManagerStartupIdentityReconcile` so the startup scan only processes persistent sessions that may genuinely need identity resolution.

## Changed files

- `src/acp/control-plane/manager.startup-identity-reconcile.ts` -- Added `session.acp.mode === "oneshot"` guard after the session key check
- `src/acp/control-plane/manager.startup-identity-reconcile.test.ts` -- Regression test: oneshot session with pending identity is skipped (checked=0, no ensure/getStatus calls)

## Overlap with #85399

PR #85399 (bound startup sidecar fanout) rewrites the same startup reconcile loop. This PR's guard (`session.acp.mode === "oneshot" -> continue`) is an independent behavior concern from #85399's fanout bounding. Both changes apply cleanly on the same function. Landing order should not matter as long as the guard is preserved during any rebase.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** ACP startup identity reconciliation processes oneshot sessions unnecessarily, producing misleading "checked=N resolved=0 failed=N" warnings and making ensure/reconcile calls for ephemeral sessions that will never need identity resolution.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v26.3.0, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/wt-86711`, gateway started on a real OpenClaw deployment at `~/.openclaw`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Built from patched branch: `CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`

  Step 2. Injected stale oneshot and persistent ACP sessions with proper pending identity shape (including `acpxSessionId` for stable session ID matching, which is what `identityHasStableSessionId` checks before entering reconciliation):

  ```console
  $ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/state/openclaw.sqlite "
  INSERT OR REPLACE INTO acp_sessions
    (session_key, session_id, backend, agent, runtime_session_name,
     identity_json, mode, state, last_activity_at, updated_at)
  VALUES
    ('agent:main:acp:oneshot-proof-1','b9628345-9622-4d13-b558-b93110aa33c8',
     'embedded','main','oneshot-proof-1',
     '{\"state\":\"pending\",\"source\":\"ensure\",\"acpxSessionId\":\"acpx-stale-1\",\"lastUpdatedAt\":1700000000}',
     'oneshot','completed',1700000000,1700000000),
    ('agent:main:acp:oneshot-proof-2','b9628345-9622-4d13-b558-b93110aa33c8',
     'embedded','main','oneshot-proof-2',
     '{\"state\":\"pending\",\"source\":\"ensure\",\"acpxSessionId\":\"acpx-stale-2\",\"lastUpdatedAt\":1700000000}',
     'oneshot','completed',1700000000,1700000000),
    ('agent:main:acp:persistent-proof-1','b9628345-9622-4d13-b558-b93110aa33c8',
     'embedded','main','persistent-proof-1',
     '{\"state\":\"pending\",\"source\":\"ensure\",\"acpxSessionId\":\"acpx-stale-3\",\"lastUpdatedAt\":1700000000}',
     'persistent','pending',1700000000,1700000000);
  "
  ```

  Step 3. Verified the rows are in the live state DB:

  ```console
  $ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/state/openclaw.sqlite "SELECT session_key, mode, json_extract(identity_json,'$.acpxSessionId') FROM acp_sessions;"
  agent:main:acp:oneshot-proof-1|oneshot|acpx-stale-1
  agent:main:acp:oneshot-proof-2|oneshot|acpx-stale-2
  agent:main:acp:persistent-proof-1|persistent|acpx-stale-3
  ```

  Step 4. Compared compiled production bundles (before vs after) to show the exact code change.

  Step 5. Started the patched gateway and ran reconcile tests.

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  **Before (upstream/main `dist/manager-rPb2hqbU.js`):** No oneshot filter in the reconcile loop. All sessions with pending identity + stable session ID (acpxSessionId) enter reconciliation:

  ```console
  $ sed -n '1449,1452p' /tmp/oc-base/dist/manager-rPb2hqbU.js
  for (const session of acpSessions) {
  if (!session.acp || !session.sessionKey) continue;
  const currentIdentity = resolveSessionIdentityFromMeta(session.acp);
  if (!isSessionIdentityPending(currentIdentity) || !identityHasStableSessionId(currentIdentity)) continue;
  ```

  **After (patched `dist/manager-mG2xJXhX.js`):** Oneshot sessions are skipped before the identity check:

  ```console
  $ sed -n '1449,1453p' /tmp/wt-86711/dist/manager-mG2xJXhX.js
  for (const session of acpSessions) {
  if (!session.acp || !session.sessionKey) continue;
  if (session.acp.mode === "oneshot") continue;
  const currentIdentity = resolveSessionIdentityFromMeta(session.acp);
  if (!isSessionIdentityPending(currentIdentity) || !identityHasStableSessionId(currentIdentity)) continue;
  ```

  **Gateway starts cleanly with the patched module and stale oneshot rows in the state DB:**

  ```console
  $ cd /tmp/wt-86711 && timeout 15 node openclaw.mjs gateway
  [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 1.3s)
  [gateway] ready
  [shutdown] completed cleanly in 77ms
  ```

  **Test identity shape matches actual reconciliation requirements:** The test at `manager.startup-identity-reconcile.test.ts:181-185` uses the same shape: `mode: "oneshot"` with `identity: { state: "pending", acpxSessionId: "acpx-stale" }`, confirming the guard fires before `identityHasStableSessionId` would select the row for reconciliation.

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'mode.*oneshot\|acpxSessionId.*stale' /tmp/wt-86711/src/acp/control-plane/manager.startup-identity-reconcile.test.ts
  181:      mode: "oneshot",
  185:        acpxSessionId: "acpx-stale",
  ```

  **All 4 reconcile tests pass:**

  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.startup-identity-reconcile.test.ts
   ✓ |acp| manager.startup-identity-reconcile.test.ts (4 tests) 18ms
     ✓ skips oneshot sessions with pending identity
     ✓ reconciles persistent sessions with pending identity
     ✓ skips persistent sessions with resolved identity
     ✓ skips sessions without ACP metadata
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  4 passed (4)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled production bundle at `dist/manager-mG2xJXhX.js:1451` inserts `if (session.acp.mode === "oneshot") continue;` before the pending identity check. This skips the 2 stale oneshot rows (which have `acpxSessionId` set and would otherwise pass `identityHasStableSessionId`) and prevents the false `checked=N failed=N` warning. The gateway started cleanly on a real deployment with stale oneshot rows in the live state DB. The unit test creates an identical session shape (`mode: "oneshot"`, `identity.acpxSessionId: "acpx-stale"`) and asserts `checked: 0`, confirming the guard fires before reconciliation.
- **What was not tested:** A multi-backend ACP deployment with real Codex/Claude agent sessions where the stale oneshot count is large (7+). The test used injected rows with correct identity shape rather than organically accumulated sessions. The core behavior (oneshot sessions skipped during reconciliation) was verified with real compiled bundle comparison, real gateway startup, and real state database rows containing proper `acpxSessionId` values.
